parsed_review.summary: CP00-295 is a descriptor-only RP08 email/DMS Hermes binding slice pack (risk B, 40 units, RP08.P08.M04.S13-RP08.P08.M06.S08) and passes read-only review with no blocking findings. The CP295 pack binding (registry.js:415), requirements (registry.js:2192), no-write attestation (registry.js:2186), contract projection, validator script (scripts/validate-rp08-email-dms-core-contract.mjs:1148-1175), tests (packages/email-dms/test/model.test.js:1506-1553), README, and manifest are mutually consistent. Deliverable distribution {implementation:18, hermes_evidence:19, test:2, claude_review:1} and micro-title distribution {Secondary Workflow Slice:10, Permission And Audit Binding:22, Synthetic Fixture Set:8} reconcile with the baseline included_units and prompt. All 7 required public exports exist and are re-exported via index.js. Descriptor-only/no-runtime/no-real-data/no-leak boundaries are enforced in code (freezeCp295Result at service.js:3250 plus strict !== false guards in validateEmailDmsCoreCp295HermesBindingSliceDescriptor at validators.js:5353), not merely declared. Authority boundary is preserved: claude_is_final_approval=false, human final approval required, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation; pack-level production_ready=false with claude review status pending. Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic descriptor/evidence-packet/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, executes_command_runtime=false), not product runtime. CP178+ review hardening is supported: forbidden_review_evidence (registry.js:598) rejects auth-failure, zero-byte, malformed-JSON, fenced-JSON, tool-call-shaped, and missing-field evidence, and the readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON. Unrelated dirty/untracked files (including docs/ldip-integration/claude-review-results/c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json) are correctly scoped out and not treated as this pack's review evidence. Post-review gated commands (closeout-pack:validate, closeout-pack:generate) are appropriately deferred per the hardened review sequence.
